title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture
summary: Applications Opening Soon for One of Canada s Most Prestigious Fully Funded Graduate Scholarship Programs for African Students The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University is now preparing to launch its 2027 application cycle, offering a transformative opportunity for talented young African leaders to pursue fully funded master s studies at one of Canada s most prestigious universities. Hosted by McGill University in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, this highly competitive scholarship program is designed to support outstanding African students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement, leadership potential, commitment to community service, and a strong desire to contribute to the social and economic development of Africa. The scholarship is far more than a financial award. It is a comprehensive leadership and educational initiative that empowers scholars to become agents of change capable of transforming sectors such as public health, nutrition, public policy, food systems, environmental sustainability, and agriculture across the African continent. Each year, the program awards approximately 35 fully funded scholarships across 13 eligible graduate programs, covering all major educational and living expenses while providing leadership development opportunities and access to one of the world s leading academic communities. About the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program is a global initiative that seeks to develop the next generation of ethical and transformative leaders. At McGill University, the program specifically focuses on preparing African scholars to address critical development challenges in: Sustainable agriculture Food security Public health Nutrition Environmental management Water resources Biotechnology Public policy The program recognizes that Africa s future depends heavily on investing in talented young people who can drive innovation, influence policy, strengthen institutions, and improve the well-being of communities across the continent. By combining academic excellence with leadership training, community engagement, and global networking opportunities, the scholarship creates pathways for scholars to make lasting contributions both during and after their studies. What Makes the McGill Mastercard Foundation Scholarship Unique? Unlike many traditional scholarships that focus only on tuition support, the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program offers a holistic support system designed to remove financial barriers and help scholars succeed academically, professionally, and personally. The scholarship places strong emphasis on: Leadership development Community impact Social responsibility Academic excellence Sustainable development Long-term contribution to Africa Successful scholars become part of a global network of Mastercard Foundation Scholars and alumni working to create positive change in their communities and countries. Scholarship Benefits The Mastercard Foundation Scholarship at McGill University is fully comprehensive and covers virtually all expenses associated with graduate studies in Canada. Benefits include: Full Tuition Coverage The scholarship covers the complete international student tuition fees required to pursue an eligible master s degree at McGill University. On-Campus Housing Scholars receive accommodation support through on-campus housing arrangements, helping ensure a smooth transition into university life. Monthly Living Stipend Recipients receive financial support to cover living expenses throughout their studies. Academic Resources and Learning Support The program provides access to academic tools, learning materials, and support services designed to maximize academic success. Pre-Arrival and Immigration Support Scholars receive guidance and support throughout: Immigration procedures Study permit preparation Travel planning Arrival and settlement processes Leadership Development Opportunities Participants gain access to: Leadership training Mentorship opportunities Professional development activities Networking events Community engagement initiatives Access to a Global Scholars Network Students become members of an international community of Mastercard Foundation Scholars, opening doors to lifelong collaborations and opportunities. Number of Scholarships Available McGill University offers: 35 Scholarships Annually These scholarships are distributed across 13 eligible graduate programs. Competition is expected to be intense due to the program s prestige and comprehensive funding package. Eligible Academic Programs The scholarship supports selected master s programs that align with Africa s development priorities. Non-Thesis Programs Eligible non-thesis programs include: Sustainable Agriculture Animal Science: Sustainable Agriculture Environmental and Water Resources Engineering Bioresource Engineering – Environmental Engineering Bioresource Engineering – Integrated Water Resource Management Food Systems and Biotechnology Biotechnology Food Science Food Safety Integrated Food Bioprocessing Health and Public Health Human Nutrition (Project) Biostatistics Public Health Public Policy Public Policy Thesis-Based Programs Eligible thesis programs include: Animal Science Bioresource Engineering Important Note for Thesis Applicants Applicants interested in thesis-based programs must not contact professors or supervisors directly. Instead: Apply through the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program. Wait for scholarship screening results. If selected for a fee waiver, McGill will provide instructions regarding supervisor matching. Who Can Apply? The scholarship targets exceptional African students who demonstrate both academic excellence and a commitment to creating positive social impact. Applicants must: Academic Requirements Hold a bachelor s degree or recognized equivalent. Meet McGill University s graduate admission requirements. Have a degree closely related to the chosen graduate program. Demonstrate strong academic performance. Applicants with First Class Honors or equivalent qualifications are considered especially competitive. Citizenship Requirements Applicants must: Be citizens of an African country. Currently reside in Africa. Age Requirement Applicants must be: 35 years old or younger at the time of application. Leadership and Service Candidates should demonstrate: Community involvement Leadership experience Volunteerism Civic engagement Service initiatives Financial Need Applicants must clearly demonstrate that financial barriers have limited or prevented access to educational opportunities. Commitment to Africa Strong candidates will show: A desire to contribute to their communities. Commitment to Africa s development. Plans to create positive impact after graduation. Priority Groups The program strongly encourages applications from: Women Women applicants are given priority consideration. Persons with Disabilities Applicants living with disabilities are encouraged to apply. Displaced Populations Priority is given to: Refugees Asylum seekers Internally displaced persons (IDPs) Returnees Ineligible Applicants The following individuals are not eligible: Applicants who have already completed a master s degree. Applicants currently enrolled in a master s degree. Permanent residents of Canada. Permanent residents of the United States. Permanent residents of the United Kingdom. Permanent residents of EU countries. Dual citizens with these residency privileges. Eligible Countries The scholarship is open to citizens and residents of every country in Africa, including: Ghana Nigeria Kenya Uganda Rwanda Ethiopia South Africa Tanzania Zambia Zimbabwe Senegal Morocco Algeria Egypt And all other African Union member states. Learn more about the Eligibility HERE Mandatory Information Sessions One of the most important requirements of the application process is attendance at an official information session. Is Attendance Mandatory? Yes. Only eligible applicants who attend an information session will receive access to the application form. Failure to attend an information session means you cannot apply. Information Session Schedule North Africa West Africa June 23, 2026 June 25, 2026 East Africa Southern Africa June 30, 2026 July 2, 2026 Central Africa July 7, 2026 July 9, 2026 Open Session for All Regions July 15, 2026 Applicants should register for the session corresponding to their region. Technology Barriers Applicants unable to attend because of internet or technology challenges may submit a technological barrier disclosure form. Learn more about the Info Sessions HERE Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Academic Merit Applicants must be academically competitive for admission to McGill University. Leadership Potential The committee seeks transformative leaders capable of driving meaningful change. Community Service Applicants should demonstrate active engagement in community development initiatives. Development Impact Strong consideration is given to candidates whose studies can positively impact their home countries. Overcoming Barriers Applicants who have overcome: Financial barriers Social barriers Personal challenges Health-related challenges may receive additional consideration. Learn more about the Selection Criteria HERE Detailed Application Timeline Information Sessions June 23 – July 15, 2026 Scholarship Applications Open August 3, 2026 (9:00 AM EST) Scholarship Application Deadline August 21, 2026 (4:00 PM EST) No late applications will be accepted. McGill Admission Stage September – November 2026 Selected candidates receive instructions to apply to McGill University. University Application Deadline December 1, 2026 Only applicants who receive fee waivers can proceed. Admission Decisions February – March 2027 Scholarship Selection Mid-February – Early March 2027 Scholarship Results Late March – Early April 2027 Understanding the Selection Process The scholarship process involves several stages. Stage 1: Information Session Applicants attend a mandatory session. Stage 2: Scholarship Application Applicants complete the Mastercard Foundation application. Stage 3: Pre-Screening Applications undergo eligibility assessment. Stage 4: Fee Waiver Invitation Successful applicants receive fee waiver instructions. Stage 5: McGill Admission Application Candidates submit university applications. Stage 6: Admission Decision McGill evaluates academic eligibility. Stage 7: Scholarship Selection Final scholarship recipients are selected. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) What Is the Purpose of This Scholarship? The scholarship aims to support talented African leaders pursuing master s studies in fields critical to Africa s development, including public health, nutrition, public policy, and sustainable agriculture. What Does the Scholarship Cover? The scholarship covers: Full tuition Housing Monthly stipend Immigration support Academic resources Leadership development opportunities How Many Scholarships Are Available? McGill awards approximately 35 scholarships each year. Is the Scholarship Available for Undergraduate Students? No. The scholarship is exclusively for master s-level studies. Is Attendance at an Information Session Mandatory? Yes. Only attendees receive access to the application form. Can I Apply If I Don t Meet McGill Admission Requirements? No. Applicants must first meet graduate admission requirements. What Happens During Pre-Screening? McGill evaluates: Age eligibility Citizenship Residency Academic readiness Leadership profile Motivation Master s degree history How Long Does Pre-Screening Take? Applicants can expect results within 4–6 weeks. What Happens If I Pass Pre-Screening? Successful candidates receive: Application fee waivers Instructions for applying to McGill What Is Conditional Admission? Conditional admission means admission is dependent on receiving the scholarship. What Happens If I Am Admitted But Not Awarded the Scholarship? Conditional admission will be withdrawn. Applicants may reapply during future cycles. Can I Appeal Scholarship Decisions? No. All decisions are final. Visit HERE for more FAQs about the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027. Conclusion The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University remains one of the most prestigious fully funded master s scholarship opportunities available to African students. With comprehensive financial support, world-class education, leadership development opportunities, and access to a powerful global network, the program provides exceptional young African leaders with the tools needed to transform communities and drive sustainable development across the continent. For ambitious students passionate about public health, nutrition, public policy, biotechnology, food systems, environmental sustainability, and agriculture, this scholarship represents a life-changing opportunity to study at one of Canada s leading universities while preparing to become a catalyst for positive change in Africa. Discover more global Scholarship and African opportunities in OFY. The post Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at McGill University 2027: Fully Funded Master s Scholarships for African Leaders in Public Health, Public Policy, Nutrition, and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026 | Win Up to $2,000 USD for Sustainability & Social Impact Projects
summary: Young people around the world are leading innovative projects that tackle some of the planet’s biggest challenges. To support these future leaders, Young Planet Leaders is offering the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship, a funding opportunity designed for students and young innovators dedicated to creating meaningful social and environmental impact. This sponsorship program recognizes young individuals who are actively working toward solutions in areas such as climate change, sustainability, environmental protection, social justice, community development, and other important global causes. Through quarterly funding awards, selected applicants can receive financial support to expand, improve, and continue their impactful initiatives. About the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship The Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship is an initiative by Young Planet Leaders aimed at empowering the next generation of environmental and social impact leaders. The program seeks applicants who demonstrate: Innovation and creativity Commitment to sustainability and social good Leadership in community or environmental projects Passion for solving real-world challenges Active involvement in meaningful causes This sponsorship is specifically intended to help recipients further develop projects or organizations they are personally involved with. The funding cannot be used for tuition fees or for-profit business activities. Sponsorship Award Amounts Each quarter, Young Planet Leaders offers three sponsorship awards: First Award – $2,000 USD Second Award – $1,500 USD Third Award – $1,000 USD A total of 12 sponsorships are awarded every year. Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet the following eligibility criteria: Age Requirement Applicants must be under 22 years old. Project or Contribution Requirement Applicants should either: Be working on their own project or organization related to: Environmental sustainability Climate action Social justice Community impact Other meaningful causes OR Be contributing significantly to an existing project or organization focused on sustainability, environmentalism, or social impact. What Kind of Projects Are Eligible? The sponsorship supports a wide range of impactful initiatives, including but not limited to: Climate action campaigns Recycling and waste management initiatives Environmental awareness programs Clean energy projects Community sustainability initiatives Conservation programs Social justice and equality projects Youth advocacy movements Educational outreach programs Sustainable technology innovations Applicants with creative, measurable, and community-driven ideas are strongly encouraged to apply. How the Sponsorship Funds Can Be Used Selected recipients are expected to use the award to strengthen or expand their impact-driven work. Examples of acceptable uses include: Purchasing project materials or equipment Organizing sustainability campaigns Expanding outreach activities Supporting nonprofit operational costs Launching educational workshops Developing environmental awareness programs Scaling community impact initiatives The funds should not be used for: Tuition payments Personal academic expenses For-profit companies or commercial business ventures Application Deadlines The Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship accepts applications four times each year. Submission Deadlines: March 15 June 15 September 15 December 15 Applicants may apply multiple times per year if they are not selected initially. However, an individual may only receive one sponsorship award per year. Selection Process Applications are reviewed by the Young Planet Leaders scholarship committee. The committee evaluates candidates based on: Commitment to worthwhile causes Extracurricular involvement Leadership and initiative Academic achievements Innovation and creativity Potential impact of the project Intended use of sponsorship funds Successful applicants are generally notified within one month after the application deadline. Why You Should Apply The Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship is more than just a funding opportunity. It is a platform that recognizes young leaders who are actively working to improve the world. Benefits of applying include: Financial support for impactful projects Recognition for sustainability and social impact work Opportunity to scale community initiatives Increased visibility for youth-led projects Motivation and encouragement for young innovators For students and young leaders passionate about making a difference, this sponsorship can help turn ideas into real-world impact. Final Thoughts Young Planet Leaders continues to support young innovators who are committed to solving global challenges through creativity, leadership, and action. If you are under 22 and actively contributing to environmental sustainability, social impact, or community change, this is an excellent opportunity to receive funding and recognition for your efforts. Learn more and apply Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application showcasing their project goals, achievements, impact, and future plans before the next submission deadline. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Young Changemaker Quarterly Sponsorship 2026 | Win Up to $2,000 USD for Sustainability Social Impact Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open
summary: Study in Morocco with a Fully Funded PhD Opportunity at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic Are you looking for a fully funded PhD opportunity in 2026? Applications are now open for more than 200 fully funded doctoral positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) in Morocco for the 2026–2027 academic year . This prestigious opportunity is offered through the Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) , UM6P s dedicated doctoral training hub that promotes excellence, innovation, and multidisciplinary research. The university welcomes talented researchers from Morocco, Africa, and around the world who are eager to contribute to solving some of the most pressing global and regional challenges. About the Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) The Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic is committed to developing the next generation of researchers, innovators, and thought leaders. CEDoc provides doctoral candidates with: High-quality academic supervision Access to world-class research facilities Interdisciplinary research opportunities Collaboration across multiple research centers and institutes Mentorship from internationally recognized experts An innovation-driven research environment The program is designed to equip PhD candidates with the skills, knowledge, and experience needed to excel in academia, industry, policy, and entrepreneurship. Fully Funded PhD Research Areas Available The 2026–2027 PhD call includes opportunities across numerous research units and disciplines. Africa Business School (ABS) Research topics include: Dynamic Pricing under Uncertainty and Market Power Designing Better Organizations through Behavioral Science, Collective Intelligence, and Artificial Intelligence Digital Technologies and Women Entrepreneurship in Morocco Climate Impacts on Household Finance Artificial Intelligence and Economic Power: Utopia or Dystopia? Africa Center of Manufacturing Excellence (ACME) Research topics include: Multi-Modal NDT Data Fusion for Structural Integrity Assessment Additive Manufacturing of Structured Catalysts and Sorbents Advanced Lightweight Sandwich Structures for Aerospace Applications AI-Based Multi-Sensor Inspection for Composite Structures Explainable Digital Twins for Predictive Maintenance College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES) Research topics include: Hydroclimatic Shifts in North Africa Groundwater and Water Resource Management Water Cycle Dynamics in Semi-Arid Regions Climate Change Adaptation Research Sustainable Agriculture and Environmental Systems Additional opportunities are available across several other UM6P research institutes and centers, covering fields such as engineering, data science, artificial intelligence, sustainability, economics, manufacturing, environmental sciences, and emerging technologies. Who Can Apply? Applicants should hold: A Master s degree An Engineering degree Or an equivalent qualification relevant to the proposed research area Candidates from all nationalities are encouraged to apply. Application Deadline 📅 Application Deadline: July 15, 2026 Interested applicants should review the available research topics carefully before submitting their applications. Important: Candidates may apply to a maximum of two PhD subjects . Why Choose UM6P? University Mohammed VI Polytechnic has emerged as one of Africa s leading research universities, with a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, entrepreneurship, and impact-driven research. Benefits of joining UM6P include: Access to cutting-edge research infrastructure Collaboration with leading international researchers Strong industry and institutional partnerships A vibrant international academic community Opportunities to contribute to Africa s development and global scientific advancement How to Apply Prospective applicants can explore the available doctoral research topics and submit their applications through the official UM6P doctoral admissions portal. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these positions. Final Thoughts For aspiring researchers seeking a fully funded PhD in Africa, the University Mohammed VI Polytechnic s 2026–2027 doctoral recruitment campaign presents an exceptional opportunity. With over 200 funded positions spanning diverse disciplines, world-class research facilities, and a commitment to innovation and excellence, UM6P offers an ideal environment to pursue advanced research and make a meaningful impact. Don t miss your chance to join one of Africa s fastest-growing research institutions and advance your academic and professional career. Key Information at a Glance Institution: University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) Program: Fully Funded PhD Positions Number of Positions: 200+ Academic Year: 2026–2027 Location: Morocco Eligible Applicants: Master s degree, Engineering degree, or equivalent holders Application Limit: Maximum of 2 research subjects Deadline: July 15, 2026 For questions regarding the application process, applicants may contact: cedoc@um6p.ma How to Apply Prospective applicants can explore the available doctoral research topics and submit their applications through the official UM6P doctoral admissions portal. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these positions. The post Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions
summary: Applications Open for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme FirstBank has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, offering ambitious young graduates across Ghana and Nigeria an exciting opportunity to begin a rewarding career in banking and financial services. As one of Africa s most respected and established financial institutions, FirstBank continues to invest in developing the next generation of banking professionals through its flagship Graduate Trainee Programme. This strategic initiative is specifically designed to attract, develop, and retain exceptional graduates who will contribute to the bank s long-term growth, innovation, and transformation agenda across Africa. The programme provides participants with an opportunity to gain practical industry experience, develop leadership competencies, build professional networks, and acquire the skills necessary to thrive in the rapidly evolving financial services sector. About the FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme The Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme serves as a talent development platform aimed at identifying high-potential graduates and equipping them with the technical, professional, and leadership skills required to excel within the banking industry. The programme reflects FirstBank s commitment to nurturing young African talent and creating a pipeline of future leaders capable of driving innovation, operational excellence, and sustainable growth across the organization s Pan-African operations. Selected candidates will have the opportunity to work within a dynamic and professional environment while receiving structured training and exposure to various aspects of banking operations. Why You Should Apply The FirstBank Graduate Trainee Programme offers numerous benefits for aspiring banking professionals. Key Benefits Professional Career Development Participants gain access to structured training programs designed to accelerate professional growth and career advancement. Industry Experience Successful candidates will acquire hands-on experience within one of Africa s largest and most respected banking institutions. Exposure to Banking Operations Trainees gain valuable insights into banking operations, customer relationship management, financial services, risk management, digital banking, and business development. Networking Opportunities Participants will have opportunities to connect with experienced professionals, industry leaders, mentors, and fellow trainees across the organization. Leadership Development The programme is designed to develop future leaders who can contribute to the bank s long-term strategic objectives. Long-Term Career Opportunities Outstanding trainees may position themselves for long-term employment opportunities and career progression within FirstBank s Pan-African network. Competencies and Attributes Required FirstBank is seeking highly motivated graduates who demonstrate strong professional and personal competencies. Applicants should possess: Excellent Communication Skills Strong verbal and written communication skills are essential for effective collaboration and customer engagement. Strong Analytical Skills Candidates should be capable of analyzing information, identifying trends, and supporting business decision-making processes. Interpersonal and Relationship Management Skills The ability to build productive relationships with customers, colleagues, and stakeholders is highly valued. Self-Motivation and Results Orientation Applicants should demonstrate initiative, accountability, and a strong drive to achieve results. Innovation and Problem-Solving Ability The bank seeks graduates who can think creatively, identify opportunities, and develop practical solutions to challenges. Teamwork and Collaboration Successful candidates should be effective team players capable of working in diverse and dynamic environments. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must not be older than 28 years as of 30 June 2026. Educational Qualification Candidates must possess: A Bachelor s Degree with a minimum of Second Class Lower division; or An HND qualification with a minimum of Upper Credit. National Service Requirement Applicants must have completed their National Service Scheme (NSS) on or before 30 June 2026. What FirstBank Is Looking For The programme is designed to identify individuals who have the potential to become future leaders within the banking sector. The ideal candidate should demonstrate: Strong academic performance Leadership potential Professional integrity Adaptability and resilience Customer-focused thinking Strong work ethic Passion for banking and financial services Ability to learn quickly and embrace change Programme Locations The 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme is currently accepting applications from candidates in: Ghana Nigeria Similar programmes are also available across other African markets within the FirstBank network. Application Deadline Interested applicants must submit their applications before: 28 June 2026 Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. How to Apply Eligible candidates should submit their applications through the official FirstBank careers portal. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure that all required information is accurately completed before submission. Apply Here Final Thoughts The 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme presents an exceptional opportunity for talented graduates seeking to launch a successful career within Africa s banking and financial services sector. With access to structured learning, professional mentorship, practical experience, and exposure to one of the continent s leading financial institutions, this programme can serve as a powerful foundation for long-term career success. If you meet the eligibility requirements and aspire to build a meaningful career in banking, now is the time to apply and take the first step toward becoming part of FirstBank s next generation of leaders. For more information on this internship visit here For more internship opportunities on OFY visit here The post FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities
summary: The African Centre for Cities (ACC) at the University of Cape Town (UCT) has announced an exciting opportunity for a full-time Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) to join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) Data Programme. This position offers professionals passionate about urban development, city governance, leadership, research, and programme management the opportunity to contribute to one of Africa s most innovative city leadership initiatives. The successful candidate will play a critical role in coordinating the City Hall Fellowship Programme while simultaneously providing administrative and operational support to the AMALI Data Programme. The position is offered on a 12-month contract from approximately June 2026 to June 2027, with the possibility of renewal. Applications close on 26 June 2026 . About the African Centre for Cities (ACC) Established in 2007, the African Centre for Cities is a leading urban research institute based at the University of Cape Town. The centre is housed within the School of Architecture, Planning and Geomatics in the Faculty of Engineering and the Built Environment. ACC is recognized across Africa and internationally for its interdisciplinary approach to urban research, policy development, city leadership, and sustainable urban transformation. Drawing expertise from across the University of Cape Town, the centre addresses some of the most pressing urban challenges facing African cities today. The institution works across several thematic areas including: Urban Governance, Politics and the State Urban Health Urban Food Systems Sustainable Infrastructure Transitions Inclusive and Circular Economies Culture, Technology and Society Teaching and Learning the City Other Urban Development Themes About the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) The African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) is a flagship programme housed within the African Centre for Cities. The initiative focuses on supporting African mayors and city leaders in achieving their long-term development goals and creating lasting legacies within their cities. AMALI provides mayors with ongoing technical assistance, leadership support, skills development, and strategic advisory services throughout the course of a calendar year. A major component of the programme is the AMALI Data Programme, which supports city executives and municipal data teams in: Collecting high-quality city-level data Conducting data analysis Using evidence-based decision-making tools Improving city-scale reporting systems Strengthening urban governance through data-driven leadership The AMALI Data Programme currently consists of: A Data Programme Lead Four City Data Advisors The Project Coordinator will become an integral member of this growing and impactful team. About the City Hall Fellowship Programme One of the most exciting aspects of this position is the opportunity to oversee the City Hall Fellowship Programme. Through this initiative, Master s and PhD students are embedded in participating AMALI cities across Africa for up to ten weeks. These placements provide students with practical experience while helping city leaders address key urban challenges and advance their legacy projects. The fellowship programme creates a bridge between academic research and real-world city governance by placing talented postgraduate students directly within municipal environments. The Project Coordinator will ensure that these placements are strategically designed, effectively managed, and deliver meaningful outcomes for both students and participating cities. Key Responsibilities of the Project Coordinator 1. Coordinating the City Hall Fellowship Programme The Project Coordinator will oversee all aspects of the fellowship programme. Responsibilities include: Establishing and Managing Advisory Structures Set up an advisory group consisting of conveners from relevant Master s programmes at UCT. Coordinate regular advisory meetings. Facilitate expert guidance on programme development and implementation. Programme Alignment and Stakeholder Engagement Collaborate closely with AMALI team members and external partners. Ensure the fellowship programme supports the broader objectives of the AMALI initiative. Strengthen partnerships with participating cities and academic institutions. Student Recruitment and Selection Design and manage recruitment processes. Advertise fellowship opportunities. Coordinate application reviews. Establish selection committees where necessary. Support transparent and competitive selection processes. Placement Coordination Engage with participating AMALI cities to identify placement opportunities. Match students with appropriate city placements. Connect students with suitable advisors and mentors. Facilitate productive relationships between fellows and city stakeholders. Logistics and Operational Management Coordinate travel arrangements. Organize accommodation and placement logistics. Ensure students are effectively integrated into host city environments. Manage administrative requirements throughout the fellowship period. Monitoring and Quality Assurance Support supervisors and mentors throughout student placements. Monitor fellowship progress. Ensure fellows produce high-quality outputs that directly support city leaders legacy goals. Facilitate continuous improvement of programme delivery. Documentation and Reporting Maintain comprehensive programme records. Document successes, lessons learned, and programme outcomes. Promote the fellowship programme through communication and outreach activities. Prepare reports for ACC leadership and programme funders. Administrative Support for the AMALI Data Programme The second major responsibility of the role involves supporting the operational effectiveness of the AMALI Data Programme. The successful candidate will help ensure that programme milestones are achieved efficiently and on schedule. Responsibilities include: Programme Administration Provide administrative support to the Data Programme Lead. Support City Data Advisors in their activities. Maintain effective programme coordination systems. Assist with scheduling and programme management. Travel Coordination Organize domestic and international travel logistics. Coordinate meetings and events. Manage travel-related documentation and arrangements. Office Management Support the management of the AMALI Data Programme office. Ensure operational systems run efficiently. Assist in maintaining a productive working environment. Why This Opportunity Matters The Project Coordinator role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to urban transformation across Africa. Successful candidates will: Work with influential African mayors and city leaders. Help shape data-driven governance across African cities. Support future urban leaders through postgraduate fellowships. Engage with international urban development networks. Gain experience within one of Africa s leading urban research institutions. Contribute to evidence-based policy and city development initiatives. Working Environment The role is based within the University of Cape Town, one of Africa s highest-ranked universities and a globally respected research institution. The African Centre for Cities provides an intellectually stimulating and collaborative environment that combines academic excellence with practical impact. Professionals joining the team will work at the intersection of: Research Urban governance Public policy Leadership development Data-driven decision-making Capacity building Employment Equity and Diversity The University of Cape Town is committed to excellence, diversity, inclusion, and transformation. As a designated employer, UCT seeks to achieve its Employment Equity goals and targets and will give preference to candidates from under-represented designated groups in accordance with its Employment Equity Plan. The university actively promotes equal opportunity and encourages applications from diverse backgrounds. Application Deadline Applications for the Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) position close on: 26 June 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to review the official job advert and submit their applications before the closing date. Apply Here Final Thoughts The Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) vacancy at the African Centre for Cities represents an exceptional opportunity for professionals passionate about urban transformation, city leadership, programme management, and higher education engagement. By coordinating fellowship placements across African cities and supporting the AMALI Data Programme, the successful candidate will play a direct role in strengthening city governance, advancing data-driven decision-making, and empowering the next generation of urban leaders across the continent. For professionals seeking a meaningful career at the intersection of urban development, research, leadership, and public impact, this role offers an outstanding platform to contribute to Africa s urban future. For more information on this job opportunity visit here For more job opportunities on OFY click here The post University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 100+ Accelerator 2026: Global Startup Program Offering Funding, Mentorship, Pilot Opportunities, and Corporate Partnerships for Sustainability Innovators
summary: Applications Open for the 100+ Accelerator: Scale Your Sustainability Startup with Global Industry Leaders Applications are now open for the 100+ Accelerator, one of the world s leading sustainability-focused startup accelerator programs designed to help innovators develop, pilot, and scale transformative solutions that address some of the most pressing environmental and social challenges facing the world today. The 100+ Accelerator brings together groundbreaking startups, global corporations, investors, and sustainability leaders to accelerate innovation that can create meaningful impact across industries and supply chains. Through funding, mentorship, pilot opportunities, and strategic partnerships, the program empowers entrepreneurs to transform bold ideas into scalable solutions with global reach. If your startup is solving critical sustainability challenges and is ready to test, validate, and scale its innovation with the support of major corporate partners, the 100+ Accelerator offers an exceptional opportunity to take your business to the next level. About the 100+ Accelerator The 100+ Accelerator was established with a simple but ambitious mission: to fuel innovation and empower startups capable of solving some of the world s biggest sustainability challenges. The program believes that meaningful change happens when entrepreneurs, corporations, investors, and ecosystem partners work together to develop practical solutions that create long-term environmental and social impact. Through collaboration and innovation, the accelerator helps startups transform promising ideas into scalable businesses capable of delivering measurable results across global markets. The program s philosophy is built around three key principles: 1. Fueling Innovation Supporting entrepreneurs who are developing breakthrough technologies and business models that address critical sustainability issues. 2. Empowering Startups Providing resources, mentorship, funding opportunities, and strategic guidance needed for startup growth. 3. Scaling Impact Helping successful innovations move beyond pilot stages to create meaningful impact at scale across industries and global supply chains. Why the 100+ Accelerator Matters Organizations worldwide are increasingly seeking innovative solutions to address challenges related to: Climate change Resource scarcity Sustainable supply chains Water management Waste reduction Circular economy development Renewable energy adoption Sustainable agriculture Carbon emissions reduction Responsible manufacturing Startups often possess the creativity and agility necessary to solve these challenges but may lack the resources, partnerships, and market access needed to scale their innovations. The 100+ Accelerator bridges this gap by connecting startups directly with major corporations and industry leaders capable of supporting commercialization and growth. Program Benefits Participating startups gain access to numerous resources designed to accelerate growth and impact. Funding Opportunities Selected startups receive support to develop and implement pilot projects that demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of their solutions. Corporate Partnerships Participants collaborate with major multinational companies that can provide: Industry expertise Technical guidance Market access Pilot opportunities Strategic partnerships Mentorship and Coaching Founders receive guidance from: Corporate leaders Industry experts Investors Entrepreneurs Sustainability specialists Pilot Project Development The accelerator enables startups to test and validate their solutions in real-world operational environments. Global Visibility Selected startups gain exposure to: Investors Industry leaders Policymakers Media outlets Sustainability networks Access to a Global Innovation Community Participants become part of an international ecosystem of entrepreneurs, experts, and organizations working to build a more sustainable future. Cohort 7: Building the Future of Sustainability The accelerator recently welcomed Cohort 7, bringing together innovative founders, corporate partners, and sustainability leaders from around the world. The cohort represents a diverse group of startups working to create scalable solutions that improve sustainability across global supply chains and industries. Through collaboration, mentorship, and pilot implementation, Cohort 7 participants are helping shape a more resilient and sustainable future. Demo Day Success The program s impact continues to grow each year. At Demo Day 2025, the accelerator brought together: More than 470 founders Corporate partners Investors Sustainability professionals Industry experts The event featured: Startup pitches Industry discussions Innovation showcases Partnership opportunities Networking sessions Demo Day demonstrated the power of collaboration in accelerating sustainability innovation and scaling impactful solutions. What Types of Startups Should Apply? The 100+ Accelerator is ideal for startups working on innovations that address sustainability challenges in areas such as: Climate and Environmental Solutions Carbon reduction technologies Climate adaptation solutions Renewable energy innovations Environmental monitoring technologies Circular Economy Waste reduction systems Recycling innovations Resource efficiency technologies Sustainable materials Water Stewardship Water conservation technologies Water quality solutions Smart water management systems Sustainable Agriculture Agricultural technology Regenerative farming solutions Food system innovations Supply chain optimization Sustainable Manufacturing Resource-efficient production Green industrial processes Sustainable packaging Supply Chain Innovation Traceability systems Logistics optimization Sustainable sourcing technologies Global Network and Strategic Partners The 100+ Accelerator collaborates with a wide network of globally recognized organizations and innovation leaders. Notable ecosystem partners and collaborators include: World Economic Forum Forbes TED EY Business Insider Fast Company The Aspen Institute One Young World Female Founders Highline Beta Leaders Quest These relationships provide startups with valuable visibility and opportunities to engage with influential stakeholders. Why Startups Should Consider Applying Participating in the 100+ Accelerator can help startups: Validate their technology in real-world environments. Gain access to corporate customers. Build strategic partnerships. Increase investor readiness. Expand market opportunities. Accelerate product development. Strengthen business models. Scale impact more rapidly. How to Apply Applications are currently open. Interested startups should review the eligibility criteria and application requirements through the official 100+ Accelerator website. Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong submissions that clearly demonstrate: The sustainability challenge being addressed The uniqueness of the solution Potential for scalability Measurable impact Commercial viability Alignment with sustainability goals Apply Here Final Thoughts The 100+ Accelerator continues to be one of the most influential startup programs focused on sustainability innovation worldwide. By connecting entrepreneurs with funding, mentorship, corporate partners, and pilot opportunities, the program helps transform bold ideas into scalable solutions capable of creating lasting environmental and social impact. For startups committed to solving critical sustainability challenges and building a more resilient future, the 100+ Accelerator represents an unparalleled opportunity to accelerate growth, validate solutions, and expand impact on a global scale.
