title: GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges
summary: Applications Open for the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026 Innovators, researchers, organizations, entrepreneurs, policymakers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to apply for the prestigious GCSP Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026. Organized by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP), this internationally recognized award celebrates bold, creative, and impactful solutions that address some of the world s most pressing security challenges. Since its launch in 2015, the GCSP Prize for Innovation has become a leading platform for recognizing outstanding achievements in global security. The award honors individuals and organizations that develop innovative approaches, technologies, research, programs, and initiatives capable of improving peace, stability, resilience, and security worldwide. The competition is open to applicants from all sectors and disciplines, reflecting the growing need for interdisciplinary collaboration in addressing modern security threats. About the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) The Geneva Centre for Security Policy is an international foundation dedicated to advancing peace, security, and international cooperation. Through its educational programs, policy dialogue initiatives, research activities, and innovation-focused projects, the GCSP serves a global community of professionals, institutions, and leaders working to build a more secure world. The Prize for Innovation in Global Security is part of the GCSP s Creativity and Innovation Initiative and is managed by the organization s Geopolitics and Global Futures Department. What Is the GCSP Prize for Innovation? The GCSP Prize for Innovation recognizes exceptional achievements that contribute innovative solutions to contemporary and emerging security challenges. The award seeks to identify transformative ideas and initiatives that have the potential to make a significant impact on global peace and security. Winning projects often demonstrate originality, scalability, effectiveness, and practical relevance to today s complex security environment. The competition welcomes a broad range of innovations, including: New technologies addressing security challenges Research publications advancing security knowledge Policy innovations and governance solutions Conflict prevention initiatives Peacebuilding programs Cybersecurity innovations Humanitarian security interventions Community resilience projects Climate-security solutions Artificial intelligence applications for security Educational initiatives promoting peace and stability Institutional reforms and innovative organizational approaches Prize Benefits Successful applicants will receive international recognition and valuable opportunities to showcase their work to a global audience. First Place Winner The first-place laureate will receive: CHF 10,000 cash prize Certificate of Excellence International recognition from a leading global security institution Increased visibility among policymakers, researchers, practitioners, and security experts Second and Third Place Winners Recipients will receive: Official Certificates of Recognition International exposure and professional recognition Honourable Mentions Two additional projects will receive: Honourable Mention Awards Recognition for outstanding contributions to global security innovation Why You Should Apply Participating in the GCSP Prize for Innovation offers numerous advantages: 1. Global Visibility The award provides an international platform to showcase innovative work before experts, practitioners, governments, international organizations, and global decision-makers. 2. Financial Support The CHF 10,000 cash award can help scale impactful projects and support future innovation efforts. 3. Professional Recognition Winning or being shortlisted by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy can significantly strengthen professional credibility and organizational reputation. 4. Networking Opportunities Participants gain exposure to a global community of innovators, researchers, policymakers, and security professionals. 5. Impact Amplification The competition helps promising solutions reach wider audiences and potential partners capable of expanding their impact. Who Should Apply? The GCSP Prize welcomes applications from a diverse range of actors including: Academic researchers Universities and research institutions Non-governmental organizations Civil society organizations Technology companies Startups Government agencies International organizations Independent innovators Peacebuilders Humanitarian actors Security practitioners Policy experts Social entrepreneurs Applicants from all sectors are encouraged to submit innovative work that demonstrates measurable contributions to global security. Areas of Interest The competition is particularly relevant for projects focused on: International security Peace and conflict resolution Strategic foresight Cybersecurity Artificial intelligence and security Geopolitics Human security Climate and security Countering violent extremism Crisis management Humanitarian innovation Defense innovation Governance and resilience Emerging technologies Global futures and strategic competition Application Deadline Applicants must submit their applications no later than: 23 September 2026 at 23:59 UTC+2 Late applications may not be considered. Winners Announcement The winners of the 2026 GCSP Prize for Innovation will be announced during Fall 2026. How to Apply Interested applicants should review the official rules and regulations and submit their applications through the Geneva Centre for Security Policy application portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their submissions early and ensure that all required information is completed before the deadline. Apply Here Final Thoughts The GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026 represents a remarkable opportunity for individuals and organizations working to transform the future of global security. Whether through technological innovation, groundbreaking research, policy development, or practical peacebuilding initiatives, this award recognizes those whose work is shaping safer and more resilient societies. With a CHF 10,000 cash prize, international recognition, and the opportunity to engage with a global network of security professionals, the GCSP Prize for Innovation remains one of the most prestigious opportunities for innovators committed to advancing peace and security worldwide. If your work is creating meaningful solutions to today s most pressing security challenges, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. For more information on this competition visit here For more global opportunities on OFY, visit here The post GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD
summary: Aspiring policy professionals, researchers, economists, and international development enthusiasts now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience at one of the world s leading international organizations. The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) Middle East and Africa Division is currently seeking an intern to support the work of the division beginning in September 2026 . This internship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful policy initiatives, regional economic development programmes, and international cooperation efforts focused on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. Candidates with an interest in economic development, investment, competitiveness, entrepreneurship, and public policy are strongly encouraged to apply. While not a mandatory requirement, working knowledge of Arabic is considered a significant advantage . Interested applicants are invited to submit their CV directly to: sara.mouhoud@oecd.org About the OECD The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organization that works with governments, policymakers, and stakeholders worldwide to develop evidence-based policies that promote prosperity, equality, opportunity, and well-being. The OECD serves as a global platform for policy dialogue, research, data analysis, and international cooperation, helping countries address economic, social, and environmental challenges. Working within the OECD provides unparalleled exposure to international policymaking, research, and development initiatives that influence governments and institutions across the globe. About the Middle East and Africa Division The OECD Middle East and Africa Division plays a crucial role in strengthening partnerships and supporting policy reforms across countries in the Middle East and North Africa region. The division works closely with governments, policymakers, private sector stakeholders, development partners, and international institutions to promote: Economic growth Sustainable development Investment promotion Private sector development Entrepreneurship Job creation Economic competitiveness Youth empowerment Women s economic participation Through research, policy dialogue, technical assistance, and capacity-building initiatives, the division contributes to the economic transformation and resilience of countries throughout the region. The MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme One of the flagship initiatives supported by the OECD Middle East and Africa Division is the MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme. Established in 2005 at the request of governments across the Middle East and North Africa, the programme serves as a strategic partnership between OECD and MENA economies. For over two decades, the programme has facilitated: Knowledge exchange Policy dialogue Capacity building Sharing of international best practices Investment promotion initiatives Entrepreneurship development Economic reform support As the programme celebrated its 20th anniversary in 2025, it continues to serve as a critical platform for fostering inclusive, sustainable, and competitive economies throughout the region. Objectives of the MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme The programme operates under the mandate of the 2021 Ministerial Declaration and focuses on supporting reforms that help countries: 1. Mobilize Investment The programme supports efforts to: Improve investment climates Attract foreign direct investment Strengthen investor confidence Enhance regulatory frameworks 2. Strengthen Private Sector Development Activities focus on: Business environment reforms SME development Competitiveness enhancement Innovation promotion 3. Foster Entrepreneurship The programme supports: Startup ecosystems Entrepreneurial capacity building Innovation-driven growth Youth entrepreneurship initiatives 4. Promote Inclusive Growth A major emphasis is placed on creating opportunities for: Young people Women Emerging entrepreneurs Underserved communities Countries Participating in the Programme The MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme currently engages 18 economies: Algeria Bahrain Djibouti Egypt Iraq Jordan Kuwait Lebanon Libya Mauritania Morocco Oman Palestinian Authority Qatar Saudi Arabia Tunisia United Arab Emirates Yemen These countries collaborate with OECD member states to exchange expertise and develop policies that support economic transformation and sustainable development. How the Programme Operates The programme functions through a structured governance framework that includes: High-Level Ministerial Meetings Senior government officials and policymakers gather to: Set strategic priorities Discuss regional challenges Share policy experiences Coordinate reform efforts Annual Steering Group Meetings The Steering Group monitors: Programme implementation Progress toward objectives Future priorities Regional cooperation initiatives Regional Policy Dialogues The programme organizes policy discussions across multiple thematic work streams to encourage knowledge sharing and collaborative problem-solving. Country-Specific Projects Tailored support is provided through: Dedicated Country Programmes Bilateral initiatives Technical assistance projects Capacity-building activities Notable examples include: OECD Country Programme with Egypt Bilateral initiatives with Morocco Bilateral initiatives with Saudi Arabia What the OECD Internship Could Involve Although specific responsibilities have not been publicly detailed, interns in OECD divisions commonly contribute to: Research and Analysis Conducting background research Reviewing policy developments Analyzing economic and social data Supporting report preparation Programme Support Assisting with project coordination Supporting event organization Preparing briefing materials Contributing to programme activities Stakeholder Engagement Supporting communication with partners Assisting with outreach activities Coordinating meetings and workshops Knowledge Management Compiling research findings Drafting summaries and reports Supporting publication development Benefits of the Internship An internship with the OECD offers numerous professional advantages. International Experience Gain firsthand experience working within a globally respected international organization. Policy Exposure Develop an understanding of: Economic policymaking International cooperation Development strategies Regional reform initiatives Professional Networking Interact with: OECD experts Government officials International development practitioners Researchers Policy specialists Career Development The experience can strengthen future career prospects in: International organizations Government ministries Development agencies Think tanks Research institutions Consulting firms Multilateral organizations Why Arabic Language Skills Are Valuable The Middle East and Africa Division works extensively with stakeholders across the MENA region. As a result, candidates with working knowledge of Arabic may benefit from: Enhanced communication with regional partners Greater understanding of regional contexts Ability to engage with Arabic-language materials Increased effectiveness in programme activities However, Arabic remains an asset rather than a mandatory requirement. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Why You Should Apply An internship with the OECD Middle East and Africa Division provides a rare opportunity to contribute to meaningful policy work that supports economic transformation, entrepreneurship, investment, and sustainable development across the MENA region. For students and young professionals seeking a career in international affairs, economics, public policy, or development, this internship offers valuable exposure to global policymaking processes and the chance to work alongside leading experts shaping the future of economic cooperation and reform. Candidates interested in launching an international policy career are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Click here for more global internship opportunities The post OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France: Applications Open for Students, Young Professionals, and Researchers Driving the Energy Transition
summary: The European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 is now accepting applications for its highly anticipated fifth edition, offering an exceptional opportunity for students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders to engage directly in shaping the future of Europe s energy transition. Scheduled to take place from 26–29 October 2026 in Nice, France, the forum will bring together some of the brightest young minds from across Europe and the Mediterranean region to exchange ideas, collaborate on solutions, strengthen professional networks, and contribute to discussions that will influence the future of sustainable energy systems. Organized by the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN), EYEF has become one of Europe s leading youth platforms dedicated to empowering the next generation of energy professionals and ensuring that youth perspectives are represented in the global energy dialogue. Applications are open from 11 May to 21 June 2026, and selected participants will embark on a multi-month journey that combines virtual learning, collaboration, and networking activities before culminating in the in-person forum in Nice. About the European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) The European Youth Energy Forum is more than a conference. It is a youth-driven platform designed to connect ambitious young people who are passionate about energy, climate action, sustainability, innovation, and policy development. Since its inception, EYEF has provided a unique space where participants can: Discuss pressing energy challenges Explore innovative solutions Learn from industry experts Collaborate across disciplines Build international partnerships Develop leadership skills Contribute to Europe s energy transition agenda The forum recognizes that young people are not simply future stakeholders in the energy sector—they are already playing critical roles in shaping sustainable energy systems, influencing climate action policies, and advancing innovation across industries. EYEF 2026 Theme: From Skills to Impact The 2026 edition of the forum is built around the theme: From Skills to Impact: Empowering Young Professionals to Implement the Energy Transition This theme reflects a growing recognition that achieving energy transition goals requires more than technical knowledge alone. It requires: Leadership Collaboration Innovation Practical implementation skills Cross-sector partnerships Youth engagement The forum seeks to equip participants with the tools, networks, and experiences needed to translate knowledge into real-world impact. Why EYEF 2026 Matters Europe is undergoing one of the most significant energy transformations in history. Countries across the continent are investing heavily in: Renewable energy Green hydrogen Energy efficiency Smart grids Sustainable mobility Climate resilience Decarbonization strategies At the same time, governments, industries, and institutions increasingly recognize the importance of involving young people in decision-making processes. EYEF serves as a bridge between youth talent and the institutions driving Europe s energy future. The forum provides participants with opportunities to: Engage with industry experts Learn from policymakers Connect with researchers Collaborate with peers Develop innovative solutions A Unique International Experience One of the defining features of EYEF is its international and interdisciplinary nature. The forum welcomes participants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds, creating a rich environment for knowledge sharing and collaboration. Participants typically represent fields such as: Renewable energy Engineering Environmental sciences Climate policy Sustainable development Economics Energy law Innovation and entrepreneurship Research and academia This diversity allows participants to explore energy challenges from multiple perspectives and develop more comprehensive solutions. Building on Collaboration with IRENA The European Youth Energy Forum continues its collaboration with the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) Youth Forum. This partnership strengthens connections between regional and global youth energy initiatives and provides participants with greater exposure to international energy discussions. The collaboration contributes to: Global knowledge exchange International networking opportunities Exposure to renewable energy developments Youth participation in global energy governance What Selected Participants Can Expect Participants selected for EYEF 2026 will participate in a comprehensive programme that extends beyond the in-person event. Online Learning Journey Before arriving in Nice, participants will engage in an online programme running from July to October 2026. This phase is designed to: Introduce key energy transition topics Facilitate networking Prepare participants for the forum Encourage collaborative learning In-Person Forum in Nice The in-person event will take place from 26–29 October 2026. During the forum, participants can expect: Interactive workshops Expert discussions Collaborative sessions Networking events Team-based activities Youth-led initiatives Professional development opportunities Long-Term Network Access Participants become part of the wider European Youth Energy Network community, providing opportunities for future collaboration and engagement. Who Should Apply? EYEF is designed for motivated individuals who are passionate about creating a sustainable energy future. Ideal applicants include: Students University students studying: Energy Engineering Environmental sciences Climate studies Public policy Economics Sustainability Young Professionals Early-career professionals working in: Energy companies Utilities Government agencies NGOs Climate organizations Research institutions Researchers Researchers exploring topics related to: Renewable energy Climate change Energy systems Sustainability Energy policy Youth Leaders Young changemakers involved in: Advocacy Climate action Community engagement Youth empowerment Timeline for EYEF 2026 The forum follows a structured journey that combines preparation, learning, and collaboration. Applications 11 May – 21 June 2026 Online Programme July – October 2026 Live Event 26–29 October 2026 Location: Nice, France Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the expected high level of competition. About the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN) The forum is organized by the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN), a youth-led organization dedicated to increasing youth participation in energy discussions and supporting young professionals entering the sector. EYEN works to: Empower young professionals Strengthen youth voices Promote innovation Facilitate networking Support energy transition initiatives The organization has become a leading platform for youth engagement in Europe s energy landscape. The EYEF 2026 Organising Team The forum is coordinated by a dedicated group of volunteers and young professionals from across Europe. Key members include: Daria Alekseeva – EYEF Manager Florentina Oldani – Logistics Coordinator Melav Salih – Programme Coordinator Samantha Chinyoka – Programme Officer Javiera Branes – Programme Officer Szymon Hoffmann – Sponsorship Coordinator Together with many other volunteers, they work to ensure the success and impact of the forum. Partnerships and Collaboration EYEF 2026 is supported by institutional and industry partners committed to advancing youth participation in the energy transition. The forum continues its cooperation with IRENA and actively welcomes new partners interested in supporting youth engagement in energy and sustainability. These partnerships help provide participants with access to: Industry expertise Professional networks Learning opportunities Career development pathways Why You Should Apply Participating in EYEF 2026 can help you: Expand your professional network Learn from energy experts Develop leadership skills Gain international exposure Collaborate on innovative solutions Strengthen your career prospects Join a community of energy changemakers Whether you are just beginning your career or already working within the sector, the forum offers valuable opportunities for growth and impact. APPLY HERE Application Deadline Applications Close: 21 June 2026 Interested applicants should submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of selection. Click HERE to learn more about European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France. Conclusion The European Youth Energy Forum 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for students, researchers, young professionals, and emerging leaders who want to contribute to Europe s energy transition. Through its unique blend of online learning, international networking, professional development, and collaborative problem-solving, EYEF equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and connections needed to drive meaningful change in the energy sector. Hosted in the beautiful city of Nice, France, and supported by the European Youth Energy Network and international partners, the forum offers a powerful platform for youth engagement and leadership. Young people who are passionate about renewable energy, climate action, sustainability, innovation, and policy development are strongly encouraged to apply before the 21 June 2026 deadline. Discover more global Conference , European and Environmental Development opportunities on OFY. The post European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France: Applications Open for Students, Young Professionals, and Researchers Driving the Energy Transition appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme for Young Diplomats: Fully Immersive International Relations Training in Austria and Poland
summary: Young diplomats and emerging international relations professionals from the OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation countries now have an exceptional opportunity to gain firsthand experience within one of the world s most influential security and co-operation organizations. The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE), through its External Co-operation Section, has officially launched the call for nominations for the 2026 Study Visit Programme for OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation Young Diplomats . This prestigious two-week programme will take place from 28 September to 9 October 2026 and will provide selected participants with unique exposure to the OSCE s work, institutions, and decision-making processes. The programme will be hosted across two major European cities— Vienna, Austria , where the OSCE Secretariat is headquartered, and Warsaw, Poland , home to the OSCE Office for Democratic Institutions and Human Rights (ODIHR). With only eight participants selected for the programme, this highly competitive opportunity offers direct engagement with international diplomacy, multilateral co-operation, democratic governance initiatives, human rights institutions, media freedom mechanisms, and regional security frameworks. About the 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme The OSCE Study Visit Programme is the flagship initiative of the OSCE Secretariat s External Co-operation Section designed to strengthen engagement between the OSCE and its Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation. The programme provides young diplomats and international affairs professionals with a comprehensive understanding of the OSCE s role in promoting: Regional security Conflict prevention Democratic governance Human rights protection Rule of law Media freedom International co-operation Participants will spend two weeks engaging directly with OSCE experts, policymakers, and institutional representatives through a combination of: Briefings Presentations Interactive discussions Institutional visits Networking opportunities Participation in OSCE-related events The programme offers a rare behind-the-scenes look at how one of the world s largest regional security organizations operates. Programme Dates and Locations Programme Period 28 September – 9 October 2026 Host Locations Vienna, Austria Warsaw, Poland The inclusion of Warsaw as part of the 2026 programme represents an important component of the study visit, allowing participants to engage directly with institutions and stakeholders connected to ODIHR in its host country. What Participants Will Learn Selected participants will gain extensive exposure to the OSCE s work and operational structures. Key learning areas include: 1. Understanding the OSCE s Mandate Participants will explore: The history and evolution of the OSCE Regional security challenges Conflict prevention strategies International diplomacy mechanisms Multilateral decision-making processes Co-operative security frameworks 2. Exposure to OSCE Executive Structures Participants will have the opportunity to visit and interact with various OSCE institutions and executive structures, gaining practical insights into their mandates and operations. 3. Human Rights and Democratic Governance Through engagements with specialized OSCE bodies, participants will deepen their understanding of: Human rights promotion Democratic institution building Election observation Rule of law initiatives Good governance practices 4. Freedom of Expression and Media Freedom The programme will also introduce participants to OSCE efforts related to: Media freedom Protection of journalists Freedom of expression Information integrity Democratic public discourse 5. Parliamentary Engagement Participants will learn how parliamentary diplomacy contributes to international co-operation through interactions with representatives and offices connected to the OSCE Parliamentary Assembly. Institutions Participants Will Visit One of the most valuable aspects of the programme is direct exposure to several key OSCE institutions. These include: OSCE Secretariat The central administrative body supporting the OSCE s activities and operations across participating states and partner countries. OSCE Office for Democratic Institutions and Human Rights (ODIHR) Participants will learn about ODIHR s work in: Human rights Election observation Democratic institution development Rule of law promotion OSCE Representative on Freedom of the Media (RFoM) This institution focuses on: Monitoring media freedom Promoting freedom of expression Supporting independent journalism Addressing media-related challenges OSCE Parliamentary Assembly (OSCEPA) Vienna Office Participants will gain insight into the parliamentary dimension of OSCE activities and international diplomacy. Benefits of Participating in the Programme The programme offers numerous professional and academic advantages, including: Professional Development Exposure to international diplomacy Understanding multilateral institutions Enhanced policy knowledge Increased understanding of regional security issues Networking Opportunities Participants will be able to connect with: OSCE officials International experts Policymakers Fellow young diplomats International affairs professionals Career Advancement The programme can significantly strengthen future career prospects in: Foreign affairs ministries International organizations Diplomatic services Public policy institutions Research organizations Multilateral agencies Practical Learning Experience Participants will move beyond theoretical knowledge and gain firsthand experience of how international organizations operate in practice. Eligibility Criteria Delegations nominating candidates must ensure applicants meet all programme requirements. Candidates must: Be under the age of 35. Have completed a first-level degree from an accredited university. Demonstrate professional fluency in English, both written and spoken. Possess prior work experience in international relations, preferably in a multilateral context. Meeting the eligibility requirements does not guarantee selection, as the programme is highly competitive and limited to eight participants. Application Process For Prospective Candidates Interested applicants should: Contact their relevant OSCE Partner for Co-operation delegation. Express interest in being nominated. Complete the required application process through their delegation. Prepare supporting documents. Nominated candidates will be required to submit: A Curriculum Vitae (CV) A motivational statement explaining their interest in the programme For Delegations Delegations are responsible for forwarding nominated candidates to the OSCE Secretariat through the designated submission channels. Important Deadline Delegation Nomination Deadline 17 July 2026 at 23:59 (CEST) Late nominations may not be considered. Prospective candidates are encouraged to contact their respective delegations well in advance of the deadline to allow sufficient time for the nomination process. Why This Opportunity Matters In today s increasingly interconnected world, effective diplomacy requires a deep understanding of multilateral institutions and international co-operation mechanisms. The 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme offers a rare opportunity for young diplomats and international affairs professionals to engage directly with experts working at the forefront of security, democracy, human rights, and international co-operation. By participating in this programme, selected candidates will gain practical knowledge, expand their professional networks, and strengthen their capacity to contribute meaningfully to international diplomacy and co-operative security efforts within their respective countries and regions. How to Apply Prospective candidates should contact their relevant OSCE Partner for Co-operation delegation to begin the nomination process. Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early and ensure all required documents are ready ahead of the nomination deadline of 17 July 2026 . For more global training opportunities click here The post 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme for Young Diplomats: Fully Immersive International Relations Training in Austria and Poland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
